title: Charge against false teachers; law good if used lawfully
summary_text: Paul apostle by command God Savior—true saying: hope in living God Savior of all. Charge some teach no other doctrine; myths endless genealogies—love from pure heart good conscience sincere faith. Law good if one uses it lawfully—not for righteous but lawless.
key_points: As I urged you … remain at Ephesus that you might command certain men not to teach a different doctrine.
The goal of this command is love from a pure heart, a good conscience, and sincere faith.
But we know that the law is good, if a man uses it lawfully.
Now to the King eternal, immortal, invisible, to God who alone is wise, be honor and glory forever and ever.
evidence_phrases: command certain men not to teach a different doctrine
love from a pure heart
the law is good, if a man uses it lawfully
Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners
King eternal, immortal, invisible

title: Prayer; qualified elders; women modest
summary_text: First of all petitions prayers for all—kings—quiet godly life. One God one mediator Christ gave ransom for all. Men pray lifting holy hands; women modest not usurp authority.
key_points: I exhort first of all that petitions, prayers … be made for all men.
There is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus.
who gave himself as a ransom for all.
I desire therefore that the men in every place pray, lifting up holy hands.
Let a woman learn in quietness with full submission.
evidence_phrases: petitions, prayers … for all men
one mediator … the man Christ Jesus
gave himself as a ransom for all
lifting up holy hands
Let a woman learn in quietness

title: Overseers and deacons; mystery of godliness
summary_text: Faithful saying—desire overseer good work. Bishop blameless temperate hospitable apt teach not novice. Deacons grave not double-tongued. Church pillar truth; great mystery godliness—God manifest in flesh.
key_points: The saying is faithful: if anyone seeks the office of an overseer, he desires a good work.
The overseer therefore must be without reproach.
Deacons in the same way must be reverent.
the house of God, which is the assembly of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.
Without controversy, the mystery of godliness is great: God was revealed in the flesh.
evidence_phrases: if anyone seeks the office of an overseer
The overseer therefore must be without reproach
Deacons in the same way
the pillar and ground of the truth
God was revealed in the flesh

title: Spirit expressly says; nourish and charge
summary_text: Spirit says expressly in later times some depart faith giving heed seducing spirits—hypocrisy liars seared conscience. Good minister nourished words faith good doctrine. Train yourself godliness—bodily exercise little; godliness all things. Elders who rule well worthy double honor.
key_points: But the Spirit says expressly that in later times some will fall away from the faith.
through the hypocrisy of men who speak lies.
Be nourished in the words of the faith.
godliness is profitable for all things.
Let the elders who rule well be counted worthy of double honor.
evidence_phrases: the Spirit says expressly
some will fall away from the faith
Be nourished in the words of the faith
Train yourself for godliness
double honor, especially those who labor in the word

title: Widows; elders; Timothy’s health
summary_text: Honor widows truly widows; younger marry. Elders presiding well especially laboring word doctrine—accusation not except two three witnesses. Timothy use little wine stomach; some men’s sins evident; good works manifest.
key_points: Honor widows who are widows indeed.
Don’t receive an accusation against an elder, except at the word of two or three witnesses.
The elders who rule well be counted worthy of double honor.
Use a little wine for your stomach’s sake.
Some men’s sins are evident, preceding them to judgment.
evidence_phrases: Honor widows who are widows indeed
two or three witnesses
double honor
Use a little wine
Some men’s sins are evident
keep yourself pure

title: Contentment; fight good fight; charge rich
summary_text: Slaves honor masters; godliness with contentment—brought nothing take nothing; food clothing. Those will be rich fall temptation pierced sorrows. Fight good fight faith; keep commandment without spot. Charge rich trust uncertain riches good works.
key_points: Godliness with contentment is great gain.
For we brought nothing into the world, and we certainly can’t carry anything out.
For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil.
Fight the good fight of faith. Lay hold of eternal life.
Charge those who are rich in this present world that they not be arrogant.
evidence_phrases: godliness with contentment is great gain
we brought nothing into the world
the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil
Fight the good fight of faith
good confession in the sight of many witnesses
not be arrogant, nor have their hope in the uncertainty of riches
